patch.value: Non-blocking background search index refresh on mutations

Biggest per-command latency source with a configured semantic stack: every create/update/close AWAITS refreshSearchArtifactsForMutation inline (registration-helpers.ts:769), which embeds the changed item via Ollama (~350ms) AND rewrites the whole local vector store. Measured pm create = 1.8-2.4s in the dev repo. CPU profile: persistLanceDbLocalTable 189ms + parseLanceDbSnapshot 97ms + ~350ms idle (Ollama HTTP) + ~200ms corpus TOON decode. Sentry confirms real-world embedding timeouts (PM-CLI-A/9) blocking mutations. Make write-time refresh non-blocking: spawn a detached refresh child (telemetry-flush pattern in core/telemetry/runtime.ts is precedent) or debounce, with the reindex lock preventing concurrent corruption. Mutation returns immediately; index catches up; existing vector_index_stale warning already covers the window. Goal: mutations feel instant even with semantic search configured.
pm create/update/close return without blocking on embedding; index refreshes in background; no store corruption under concurrent mutations

Shipped non-blocking background semantic refresh. Mutations now invalidate the keyword cache synchronously (search stays correct) and dispatch the slow Ollama embed + vector-store rewrite to a detached worker (src/core/search/background-refresh.ts + dist/cli/search-refresh.js, mirroring the telemetry-flush detached-spawn pattern). The worker drains a pending-refresh queue under the existing reindex lock (corruption-safe; reindex now imports the shared REINDEX_LOCK_ID). shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground() keeps all tests/CI inline (deterministic). ALSO fixed a second dominant cost discovered via CPU profile: the pre-mutation item-format sync (migrateItemFilesToFormat, run on every mutation by default) re-parsed/re-serialized the ENTIRE corpus; now skips items already in the target format with no legacy alternate. Combined result in the 893-item dev repo: create 2581ms->239ms, update 1693ms->265ms, close->374ms. Background worker confirmed catching the index up (new item semantically searchable within ~2-3s). Telemetry validated this as the #1 real-world latency (update p95 was 15.3s, create p95 5.4s).
